zh.lifeSummary: 當Rudolf I. von Stade於1062年出生在德國薩辛-安哈爾特馬格德包格時，他的父親Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III42歲，母親Oda von Werl19歲。他於1110年之前與Richardis von Sponheim在德國結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1124年12月7日在他的家鄉享年62歲。

meanings: Cambodian: written វ៉ុន, unexplained.

variants.name: Vaughan
meanings: Welsh: from fychan, a lenited form of bychan, a diminutive of bach ‘little’. This was often used to distinguish the younger of two bearers of the same personal name, typically the son of a father with the same name.

nameMeanings.name: Stade
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Bernhard, Bertel, Erhardt, Erna, Hannelore, Kurt, Wolfgang.
German: habitational name from any of various places so named, for example on the river Elbe.

nameMeanings.name: Rudolf
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

variants.name: Rudolph
meanings: From a Latinized version, Rudolphus, of the Germanic name Hrōdwulf ( see Rolf ). It was introduced to the English-speaking world from Germany in the 19th century. Rudolf was a hereditary name among the Habsburgs, the Holy Roman Emperors and rulers of Austria, from the Emperor Rudolf I ( 1218–91 ) to the Archduke Rudolf , Crown Prince of Austria-Hungary, who died in mysterious circumstances at his country house at Meyerling in 1889 . Rudolf Rassendyll was the central character of Anthony Hope's adventure stories The Prisoner of Zenda ( 1894 ) and Rupert of Hentzau ( 1898 ), in which he is an English gentleman who bears a great physical resemblance to the King of Ruritania, to whom he is distantly related. He successfully impersonates the king for reasons of state. In the early 20th century the popularity of this name was further enhanced by the American silent-film actor Rudolph Valentino ( 1895–1926 ), born in Italy as Rodolfo di Valentina d'Antonguolla . It has not been used much in recent times, perhaps being inextricably associated with ‘the red-nosed reindeer’ of the Christmas song.

en.lifeSummary: When Rudolf I. von Stade was born in 1062, in Magdeburg, Saxony-Anhalt, Germany, his father, Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III, was 42 and his mother, Oda von Werl, was 19. He married Richardis von Sponheim before 1110, in Germany. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 7 December 1124, in his hometown, at the age of 62.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Rudolf I. von Stade est né en 1062, à Magdeburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Allemagne, son père, Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III, avait 42 ans et sa mère, Oda von Werl, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Richardis von Sponheim avant 1110, à Allemagne. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 7 décembre 1124, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 62 ans.

de.Stade, Schleswig-Holstein, Germany: Stade, Kreis Tondern, Schleswig-Holstein, Preußen, Deutschland
de.lifeSummary: Als Rudolf I. von Stade 1062 in Magdeburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Deutschland geboren wurde, war sein Vater Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III 42 und seine Mutter Oda von Werl 19. Er hat vor 1110 in Deutschland Richardis von Sponheim geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 7. Dezember 1124 im Alter von 62 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort.

Lui sposato Richardis von Sponheim prima del 1110, nel Germania. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 7 dicembre 1124, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 62 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Rudolf I. von Stade(이)가 1062년 독일 작센-아할트 막그데버그에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III(은)는 42세였고, 그의 어머니 Oda von Werl(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1110년 이전 독일에서 Richardis von Sponheim(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1124년 12월 7일 그의 고향에서 62세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Rudolf I. von Stade nasceu em 1062, em Magdeburg, Sachsen-Anhalt, Alemanha, seu pai, Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III, tinha 42 anos e sua mãe, Oda von Werl, tinha 19 anos. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Rudolf I. von Stade родился в 1062 г. в местности магдебург, сачсен-анэлт, Германия, его отцу по имени Lothar Udo von STADE von der NORDMARK III было 42 года, а матери, которую звали Oda von Werl, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Richardis von Sponheim перед 1110 г. в местности Германия. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 7 декабря 1124 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 62 лет.

Se casó con Richardis von Sponheim antes de 1110, en Alemania. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 7 de diciembre de 1124, en su pueblo, a la edad de 62 años.
